result: If I could change the world in one way, I would increase empathy, compassion, and understanding between all people. I believe that so many of the world's problems - conflict, inequality, discrimination, neglect of those in need - stem from a fundamental lack of empathy. If people could truly put themselves in others' shoes, see things from their perspective, and feel their struggles and hopes and fears, I think it would transform the way we treat each other. More compassion would lead to more kindness, more helping of those less fortunate, more open-mindedness, more forgiveness. Conflicts would be resolved through dialogue and compromise. We would take better care of the most vulnerable among us. Hate would give way to understanding. Obviously this is an idealistic dream and increasing empathy wouldn't solve all the world's ills. But I do think it would be transformative and lead to a far more humane, united, and benevolent world. That's the change I would most wish for.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
